node.title: Uber BPS interview - Any chance of clearing the round?

article.title: Uber BPS interview - Any chance of clearing the round?
article.content:
I recently had an Uber BPS round where the problem was very similar to LC 1101 – The Earliest Moment When Everyone Become Friends, but framed in a more real-world Uber context (cab sharing / users becoming friends over time).

Initially, due to the lengthy problem statement and domain-specific wording, I struggled to immediately map it to DSU (Union-Find). After some discussion, I realized that DSU was the correct approach and explained the full solution clearly before coding.

While explaining, there were a couple of moments where the interviewer didn’t seem fully satisfied with my explanation, but he still asked me to go ahead and code, saying “let’s see”.

I started implementing the solution and almost completed the code, but there were a few small fixes left (edge cases / final checks). Unfortunately, time ran out before I could fully finalize and test the code with examples.

My questions:

- In Uber BPS rounds, how critical is complete, bug-free code vs. correct approach and explanation?
- Does partially completed code (with correct logic) still have a chance if the approach is right?
- Is running the code with examples mandatory, or does Uber focus more on problem-solving and reasoning?

Would really appreciate insights from anyone who has interviewed with Uber or faced similar situations.

Thanks!

post.content: Hi, were you asked any HLD question in the BPS round ? as in mail is mentions that there will be 15 min discussion on HLD as well....
How many questions were there? Did they ask anything from resume too related to architecture and system design?
in this market with 1 open position and 10K candidates applying , you have to be the best from the rest.
Working code is a must for Uber\'s any round of the interview.

If interviewer is satisfied with your explanation and code, then you will be moved to onsite rounds(DSA + LLD) even if your code doesn\'t run. Just hope he compares your code with right approach and your approach looks similar to the right one.
Working code is must, I have seen the folks where some of test cases failed that can still work.

sorry I don't have the answer for this question

but did you implement fast Union Find ( path compression and weighted merging ) during the interview .. and how much time it took

... although I know Union Find I am not sure I can implement it fast during interview as there are generally at least 2 problems in a round of 50min interview
